overallInterpretation: The screenshot shows a browser window split vertically into two distinct viewports. The left half has a dark navy background and displays a yellow circle and a small blue square, both appearing flat and viewed from directly above, consistent with a top-down orthographic projection. The right half has a dark reddish-brown background and displays the same two objects — a blue rectangular box and a yellow ellipse — rendered from a side angle with visible 3D depth, consistent with a perspective side view. Both viewports clearly render the same scene (same two objects: one yellow, one blue) from different camera angles. All five text artifacts show passing test suites: aspect-ratio (7 tests), node-types (Camera and RenderQuad configuration output), render-graph (8 tests), render-loop (6 tests), and renderer-apis (10 tests) — all green with zero failures.

artifacts.interpretation: Browser window split into two viewports by a vertical dividing line. Left half (dark navy background): top-down orthographic view showing a yellow circle near center and a small blue square in the lower-left area — objects appear flat with no perspective distortion. Right half (dark reddish-brown background): perspective side view showing the same yellow object as an ellipse and the blue object as a 3D rectangular box — both rendered with depth foreshortening from a side camera angle. The two viewports render the same scene from different cameras.
Vitest output showing 7 passing tests for computeViewport covering stretch, letterbox, and truncate aspect-ratio modes. All tests pass in 311ms.

Console output demonstrating Camera and RenderQuad node types. Camera has renderTarget 'tv-feed', aspectRatio 16:9, letterbox mismatch mode, and recursionDepth 2. RenderQuad references the same 'tv-feed' target with dimensions 2x1.5. Default camera has no renderTarget.

Vitest output showing 8 passing tests for buildRenderOrder covering independent cameras, split screen ordering, TV-in-scene dependency ordering, hall-of-mirrors recursion, and mixed scenarios. All tests pass in 324ms.

Vitest output showing 6 passing tests for renderFrame covering single camera, dual camera ordering, texture-to-quad binding, render target setup, recursive camera passes, and viewport reset. All tests pass in 372ms.

Vitest output showing 10 passing tests for renderer render-target and viewport APIs, including render target creation/destruction, offscreen rendering, texture binding, viewport setting/resetting, split-screen support, and multi-pass nested view rendering. All tests pass in 296ms.
